extra.message: Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) vulnerability. User-controlled 'name' query parameter (line 19) is interpolated into an f-string on line 38 to build HTML content, which is then passed to Template(content).render() on line 41. An attacker can inject Jinja2 template syntax (e.g., ?name={{7*7}} or ?name={{config.__class__.__init__.__globals__['os'].popen('id').read()}}) to achieve Remote Code Execution.

cwe: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Templating Engine
CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability. The PUBLIC_IP_SERVICE_URL is loaded from an environment variable (config.py line 11) without any validation or allowlist. The requests.get() call on line 31 makes an HTTP request to this unvalidated URL on every request to the root endpoint. An attacker who can control the environment variable can force the server to make requests to arbitrary internal or external URLs, potentially leading to information disclosure or further attacks.

Hardcoded credentials found in source code. Lines 13 and 15 contain hardcoded secret values (SUPER_SECRET_NAME and SUPER_SECRET_TOKEN) instead of loading them from environment variables as indicated by the FIXME comments. These credentials are now exposed in the git history and any copy of the source code.
